growth_picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company

short_dynamics.detail: SI 0.95% (<10%) but rising +3.89%; not declining, so borderline not pass

ai_exposure.detail: Core franchise is GLP-1/incretin metabolic drugs (Mounjaro, Zepbound). AI aids drug discovery at the margin but is not a material driver or threat to the cash-flow base.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Beta 0.481 and 85% institutional ownership indicate a low-volatility, widely-held mega-cap; nothing to flag from the supplied fields.

catalysts_general: Trading 6.1% below 52-week high; up 9.3%/19.5% over 20d/50d, above both SMAs (uptrend intact)
Forward P/E 24.3 vs trailing 38.3 — earnings expected to expand materially (forward EPS 44.47 vs trailing 28.20)
Analyst mean target $1215 (~13% upside); 24 of 31 ratings buy/strong-buy
Best-in-class quality: 82.8% gross margin, 49.4% operating margin, ROE 107%

downside_scenarios.scenario: Multiple compresses to 20x forward EPS (44.47) — roughly the analyst low target ($850); GLP-1 competition/pricing pressure
Growth-scare derating to 17.5x on slowing incretin scripts

ideal_entry.reasoning: 50-day SMA ($964) is the nearest meaningful trend support; entering on a pullback to it improves risk/reward versus chasing near the high

comparables.note: Novo Nordisk — direct GLP-1 competitor; primary read-through on obesity TAM
Large-cap pharma peer, lower growth — frames LLY's premium multiple

growth_picks.analyst_take: Highest-quality name in the set: it passes 5 of 6 growth criteria with elite margins and triple-digit ROE, and the only soft spot (a tiny but rising short interest) is immaterial. Forward P/E of 24 against 55% revenue growth is reasonable for the franchise; a buy, ideally added near the 50-day rather than at the high.

growth_picks.name: Micron Technology, Inc.

ai_exposure.detail: High-bandwidth memory (HBM) and datacenter DRAM are core to AI accelerator buildouts — Micron is a direct beneficiary of the AI capex cycle.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Beta 1.92 and 41M avg daily volume signal a high-beta, heavily-traded cyclical; nothing further to flag from supplied fields.

catalysts_general: Within 0.9% of 52-week high; up 61.9%/172.9%/171.9% over 20d/50d/90d — powerful momentum but extended
Forward P/E 10.2 vs trailing 51.0; PEG 0.37 — earnings expected to surge (forward EPS 105.95 vs trailing 21.16)
Short interest declining (-3.01%), low short ratio (0.77 days)
Net cash positive ($14.6B cash vs $10.8B debt), low D/E 14.9

downside_scenarios.scenario: Memory cycle peaks; market applies trough 8x multiple to forward EPS (105.95) on cyclicality fears
Downcycle scare derates to 6x forward as DRAM/NAND pricing rolls over

ideal_entry.reasoning: 20-day SMA ($822) marks the nearest support after a parabolic run; given a ~172% 90-day move, waiting for a pullback materially de-risks entry on this high-beta cyclical

comparables.note: In input — fellow memory/storage name in the same upcycle, similar low forward P/E
Western Digital — NAND/HDD peer, frames storage cycle

growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron passes 5 of 6 criteria on the strength of a memory super-cycle, with a forward P/E of ~10 and a declining short base that screen as cheap-on-forward-earnings. The catch is timing: a name up 172% in 90 days and sitting on its high is priced for the cycle continuing, so it's a buy on conviction in the AI/HBM demand thesis but best scaled in on pullbacks toward the 20-day.

growth_picks.name: Simon Property Group, Inc.

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 16.4% > 0 but < revenue_growth 19.3% — profitable growth, no leverage

capital_efficiency.detail: return_on_equity 113.6% (>=20%); inflated by thin/leveraged REIT equity base

short_dynamics.detail: SI ~0% (<10%) but reported change +52.94% on tiny share counts (374→572) — noise, not a real signal; declining test not met

ai_exposure.detail: Physical retail/mixed-use REIT. E-commerce is a structural headwind but premium-mall occupancy is stable; AI specifically is neither a meaningful driver nor an acute threat.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Current ratio 0.196 and D/E 457% are typical of a leveraged REIT structure; nothing additional to flag from supplied fields.

catalysts_general: 2.5% below 52-week high, trading near both SMAs — consolidating after a 14% 50-day move
Forward P/E 30.3 vs trailing 14.2 — forward EPS (6.71) well below trailing (14.37), implying earnings step-down (FFO vs GAAP EPS dynamics typical for REITs)
4.32% dividend yield, 60% payout — income component to total return
Analyst mean target $214 (~5% upside); ratings skew hold (12 of 21)

downside_scenarios.scenario: Rate-sensitivity/retail-traffic concerns compress the forward multiple; approaches analyst low ($194) and below
Retest of 52-week low on a REIT/rate selloff

ideal_entry.reasoning: Pullback below the 50-day ($199) toward prior support lifts the dividend yield above ~4.7% and improves the income-plus-modest-growth setup

comparables.note: Realty Income — triple-net retail REIT, frames yield comparison

growth_picks.analyst_take: On a growth lens SPG is a 4.5/6 that earns its points more on margin and a leverage-inflated ROE than on actual growth — revenue and earnings both sit in the borderline mid-teens. It's a solid income REIT, but the rising forward P/E (forward EPS below trailing) and hold-skewed analyst base argue for watchlist, with a better entry on a pullback that fattens the 4.3% yield.

growth_picks.name: Fair Isaac Corporation

capital_efficiency.detail: return_on_equity not provided; book_value is negative (-$90.22) and price_to_book -13.0 from heavy buybacks, so ROE is not meaningful from supplied fields

short_dynamics.detail: SI 9.21% (<10%) but rising +21.07%; not declining and not >10%, so borderline

ai_exposure.detail: FICO Score is a regulatory/network-effect moat that AI does not easily disrupt; the Software/analytics segment can be augmented by AI. Net: AI is more augmentation than threat, but not a primary growth driver.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Negative book equity reflects an aggressive buyback model (asset-light analytics), not distress; nothing further to flag from supplied fields.

catalysts_general: 41.2% below 52-week high — largest drawdown in the set despite strong fundamentals; rebounding (+10%/+18% over 20d/50d) but still -20% over 90d
Forward P/E 21.7 vs trailing 37.2; PEG 0.84 — growth meaningfully cheaper on forward earnings (forward EPS 54.16 vs trailing 31.60)
Analyst mean target $1532 (~30% upside); 15 of 21 buy/strong-buy
Elite economics: 84% gross margin, 58% operating margin, asset-light scoring franchise

downside_scenarios.scenario: Mortgage-volume/score-pricing concerns derate to 16x forward EPS (54.16); revisits the 52-week low (~$870) and analyst low ($707) zone
Deeper growth-scare compression to 14x forward on regulatory or pricing pushback

ideal_entry.reasoning: 50-day SMA ($1094) is the nearest support; given the 41% drawdown and rising short interest, entering on a hold of the 50-day balances the discount against ongoing volatility

comparables.note: S&P Global — analytics/ratings peer with a comparable data-moat
Moody's — moat/quality comp for premium analytics multiples

growth_picks.analyst_take: FICO passes every growth criterion it can be scored on (4 of 5, capital efficiency unscoreable due to buyback-driven negative equity) and trades 41% off its high at a sub-1 PEG — an unusual combination of moat quality and discount. The rising short interest and mortgage-cycle sensitivity are the watch-items, so it's a buy with the recognition that the negative-equity structure means leverage/buyback discipline must be monitored from fuller financials.

growth_picks.name: Incyte Corporation

short_dynamics.detail: SI 9.52% (<10%) AND declining -7.8%, but not <5%, so borderline not pass

ai_exposure.detail: Commercial-stage biopharma (Jakafi, Opzelura). AI can aid pipeline discovery but the near-term thesis rests on existing drug franchises and clinical readouts, not AI.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Fortress balance sheet ($4.0B cash vs $39M debt, current ratio 3.68) and short ratio 7.59 days noted; nothing further to flag from supplied fields.

catalysts_general: 12.7% below 52-week high; flat over 90d, hovering near both SMAs — range-bound consolidation
Forward P/E 10.8 vs trailing 13.8; PEG 0.36 — cheap on growth (forward EPS 9.07 vs trailing 7.08)
Short interest declining (-7.8%) but still elevated at 9.5% with a high 7.6-day cover ratio
Net-cash fortress: $4.0B cash vs $39M debt; ROE 30.8%

downside_scenarios.scenario: Jakafi patent-cliff/competition concerns compress to 9x forward EPS (9.07)
Pipeline setback drives a retest of the 52-week low

ideal_entry.reasoning: A pullback toward the lower end of the recent range (below the 50-day ~$96) offers a better margin of safety given the elevated short ratio and patent-cliff overhang

comparables.note: Vertex — profitable specialty biopharma quality comp

growth_picks.analyst_take: Incyte screens cheap (forward P/E ~11, PEG 0.36) with a fortress balance sheet and strong earnings acceleration, but on the growth framework it clusters in borderline territory — mid-teens-to-20s revenue growth and a sub-65% gross margin keep it at 4.0/6. With Jakafi patent-cliff overhang and a hold-skewed analyst base, it's a watchlist value-with-optionality name rather than an outright growth buy.

growth_picks.name: Sandisk Corporation

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth not provided in input; cannot compare against revenue_growth

short_dynamics.detail: SI 9.63% (<10%) AND declining -6.31%, but not <5%, so borderline not pass

ai_exposure.detail: NAND flash storage demand is lifted by AI datacenter buildouts and high-capacity SSDs; Sandisk is a direct beneficiary of the same storage upcycle driving Micron.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Net-cash position ($3.7B cash vs $207M debt) and current ratio 4.78 noted; the 52-week range ($37.33-$1861) reflects the recent Western Digital spinoff/re-listing, so trailing range metrics are distorted.

catalysts_general: Within 1.6% of 52-week high; up 29.9%/160.7%/223.8% over 20d/50d/90d — parabolic, highly extended
Forward P/E 10.3 vs trailing 62.5 — earnings expected to surge (forward EPS 177.84 vs trailing 29.30)
Short interest declining (-6.31%), very low short ratio (0.58 days)
Net-cash balance sheet ($3.7B cash vs $207M debt), ROE 39.3%

downside_scenarios.scenario: Memory/NAND cycle peaks; trough 8x multiple applied to forward EPS (177.84) on cyclicality fears
Mean-reversion to the 50-day SMA (~$1141) as the parabolic move unwinds

ideal_entry.reasoning: 20-day SMA ($1525) is the nearest support after a 224% 90-day move; entering near a high that's risen this fast carries poor risk/reward, so a pullback to the 20-day (or deeper to the 50-day ~$1141) is preferable

comparables.note: In input — fellow memory name in the same upcycle, nearly identical forward P/E and short dynamics
Western Digital — former parent/HDD-NAND peer; relevant given the recent spinoff

growth_picks.analyst_take: Sandisk shows blistering revenue growth and a cheap ~10x forward multiple on the NAND upcycle, but it scores 4.0/6 because earnings_growth is missing (can't confirm acceleration) and it sits up 224% in 90 days at the very top of its range. The fundamentals and AI-storage tailwind are real, yet the parabolic extension and spinoff-distorted history make this a watchlist-on-pullback rather than a chase at the high.
